0.title: Knowledge Hub Foundations
0.abstract: Knowledge Hub Foundations curates the first expansion wave of Electronic Artefacts knowledge records around graph systems, preservation, generative systems, signal, audio and public semantic publishing.

1.title: Contextual Execution
1.definition: Contextual execution is the practice of making identity, state, permissions, relationships and operating context active constraints on what a system can do.
1.abstract: Contextual execution describes runtime behavior where an operation is evaluated through entity identity, graph relations, permissions, temporal state and intended scope.

2.title: Creative Coding
2.definition: Creative coding is the use of programming as an artistic, design or exploratory medium rather than only as an engineering implementation tool.
2.abstract: Creative coding connects software, visual culture, sound, interaction, generative systems, education and experimental publishing.

3.title: Cybernetic Feedback
3.definition: Cybernetic feedback is the circulation of information about a system's state back into the system so that behavior can be regulated, adapted or transformed.
3.abstract: Cybernetic feedback connects control, communication, systems theory, generative art, machine listening, adaptive interfaces and runtime governance.

4.title: Digital Preservation
4.definition: Digital preservation is the long-term work of keeping digital objects understandable, authentic, usable and accessible despite changes in formats, software, hardware and context.
4.abstract: Digital preservation connects file formats, metadata, provenance, storage, rights, migration and interpretation into a long-term care practice for digital culture.

5.title: Entity Identity
5.definition: Entity identity is the durable naming of a person, concept, project, program, publication or artefact so that references remain stable as descriptions evolve.
5.abstract: Entity identity gives knowledge systems a way to cite things without confusing names, pages, files, versions and changing descriptions.

6.title: Generative System
6.definition: A generative system is a rule-based, procedural or computational arrangement that produces variable outputs from defined constraints, materials and processes.
6.abstract: Generative systems connect creative coding, cybernetics, algorithmic composition, simulation, design tools and AI by shifting authorship toward rules, feedback and selection.

7.title: Graph Runtime
7.definition: A graph runtime is an execution environment in which addressable entities and typed relationships participate directly in contextual computation.
7.abstract: Electronic Artefacts uses graph runtime to describe systems where entities, relationships, identity, context and events form an executable structure rather than a passive data model.

8.title: Knowledge Graph
8.definition: A knowledge graph is a structured network of entities, identifiers and typed relationships that makes knowledge navigable, queryable and reusable across contexts.
8.abstract: Electronic Artefacts uses knowledge graph to describe public knowledge systems where concepts, projects, programs, publications, sources and artefacts keep stable identities and typed relations.

9.title: Linked Data
9.definition: Linked Data is a web publishing approach that identifies things with stable URIs and connects them through machine-readable statements.
9.abstract: Linked Data gives public knowledge systems a way to make entities, pages, references and datasets interoperable across sites and software systems.

10.title: Provenance
10.definition: Provenance is information about the people, activities, sources, transformations and decisions involved in producing a thing or piece of data.
10.abstract: Provenance helps readers assess reliability, rights, authorship, lineage, context and trust across research, archive, software and cultural records.

11.title: Signal Archaeology
11.definition: Signal archaeology is the interpretation of technical, sonic, visual or archival traces as evidence of hidden processes, transformations and histories.
11.abstract: Signal archaeology gives Electronic Artefacts a way to connect audio analysis, media residue, archival provenance, memory and cultural interpretation.

12.title: Electronic Artefacts
12.abstract: Electronic Artefacts is an independent creative technology studio, software laboratory, research organization, cultural platform, label and publishing system.

13.title: ORETH
13.abstract: ORETH is an Electronic Artefacts audio intelligence and artistic research program concerned with signal analysis, pattern detection, memory, machine listening and experimental music production.

14.title: VASTE
14.abstract: VASTE is the primary proprietary runtime developed and commercialized by Electronic Artefacts for graph architectures, contextual execution, identity systems and modular world construction.

15.title: Palimpsests
15.abstract: Palimpsests is an Electronic Artefacts cultural project and conceptual album cycle connected to ORETH, memory, inheritance, transmission, entropy, signal and layered artistic production.

16.title: Vestiges
16.abstract: Vestiges is a VASTE-powered knowledge infrastructure platform designed to map, preserve, connect, transmit and activate human know-how.

17.title: Contextual Execution and Graph Runtimes
17.abstract: This article explains contextual execution as a runtime principle for systems where identity, relations, permissions and state influence what operations can occur.

18.title: Digital Preservation and Living Archives
18.abstract: This article explains digital preservation as an active cultural practice that keeps digital objects, metadata, provenance and interpretation usable over time.

19.title: Foundational Lineage #001
19.abstract: This research note records early architecture, communication-system and documentation threads that led to Electronic Artefacts, Runtime Theory and VASTE.

20.title: Generative Systems, Cybernetics and Creative Coding
20.abstract: This article traces generative systems from cybernetic feedback and algorithmic art to creative coding, sound design and contemporary AI-assisted production.

21.title: Knowledge Graphs for Cultural Infrastructure
21.abstract: This article explains how knowledge graphs support cultural infrastructure by giving people, works, sources, concepts, projects and archives stable identities and explicit relations.

22.title: Linked Data and Public Knowledge Pages
22.abstract: This article explains how public knowledge pages can use stable URLs, JSON-LD, RDF-oriented identifiers and internal links to become useful to humans, search engines and AI systems.

23.title: Signal Archaeology, Audio Memory and Machine Listening
23.abstract: This article explains signal archaeology as a way to read sonic and technical traces through audio analysis, provenance, memory and machine listening.

24.title: Runtime Theory
24.abstract: Runtime Theory investigates the minimum identities, relations, contexts and event primitives required for a coherent executable system.

25.title: JSON-LD
25.abstract: JSON-LD is a W3C format for serializing Linked Data in JSON, making semantic graph metadata compatible with common web programming workflows.

26.title: RDF
26.abstract: RDF is a W3C graph data model for representing information as subject-predicate-object statements about resources.
